prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response:
It’s tricky to pinpoint something specific about you as a stranger, since I don’t have any personal context, but I’ll go with a common misconception that many people hold. Based on general patterns in how folks interact with the world, you might be wrong about the idea that humans only use 10% of their brains at any given time.

This myth has been popularized by movies and pseudoscience, but it’s not accurate. Brain imaging studies, like those from fMRI scans, show that we actually use nearly all of our brain throughout the day, with different regions lighting up for various tasks. The 10% idea likely stems from early misunderstandings of neuroscience. It’s a fun one to debunk because it highlights how persistent myths can be! If this doesn’t resonate, feel free to share more, and I can refine my guess. 😊
